zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Bigod於1370年出生在英國越可爾澤特林頓時，她的父親John Bigod36歲，母親Amy Settrington31歲。她於1401年與Walter de Claverley結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1423年6月在英國英格蘭韋斯特 越可爾卡爾福利享年53歲。

nameMeanings.name: Bigod
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Jaroslaw, Jozef, Krystyna, Slawomir.

Polish and Jewish (from Poland): from bigos, formerlydenoting anything that was chopped or slashed, now a particular dishcomposed of hash and sauerkraut; also ‘confusion’; hence perhaps anickname for someone who caused confusion.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Bigod was born in 1370, in Settrington, Yorkshire, England, her father, John Bigod, was 36 and her mother, Amy Settrington, was 31. She married Walter de Claverley in 1401. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in June 1423, in Calverley, West Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 53.

Elle a épousé Walter de Claverley en 1401. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en juin 1423, à Calverley, West Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Bigod(이)가 1370년 영국 요르크실르 세트링턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Bigod(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Amy Settrington(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1401년 Walter de Claverley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1423년 6월 영국 잉글랜드 웨스트 요르크실르 캘벌리에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Bigod родилась в 1370 г. в местности сеттрингтон, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Bigod было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Amy Settrington, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Walter de Claverley в 1401 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла июнь 1423 г. в местности калвирли, вест еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет.
